name: Tabouleh (Burghul and Parsley Salad)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place burghul in a bowl and cover with cold water. Leave to soak for 30 minutes.
2. Drain the burghul through a fine sieve, pressing gently with the back of a spoon to extract moisture.
3. Spread the drained burghul onto a cloth and leave to dry further.
4. Wash parsley well, shake off excess moisture, remove thick stalks, wrap in a tea towel, and place in refrigerator to crisp and dry.
5. Add spring onions to the burghul and squeeze the mixture gently so that the burghul absorbs the onion flavor.
6. Chop parsley coarsely and add to the burghul with mint.
7. In a separate bowl, beat olive oil with lemon juice and stir in salt and pepper.
8. Add the dressing to the salad and toss well.
9. Peel and seed tomatoes, cut into dice, and gently stir into the salad.
10. Cover and chill for at least 1 hour before serving.
11. Serve in a salad bowl lined with crisp lettuce leaves. The lemon juice and salt mixture may be added according to individual taste. Serves 6-8.
